issues.title: Auto truncate for vertex embedding is broken by TokenCountBatchingStrategy
issues.body:
**Bug description**

I try to use vertex embedding and did test big document. I did set the auto-truncate to true. 
This correspond to this options: https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-ai/blob/be0f9fbb676627ec6f64fb99ad9c9cf407ba8941/models/spring-ai-vertex-ai-embedding/src/main/java/org/springframework/ai/vertexai/embedding/text/VertexAiTextEmbeddingOptions.java#L67

But I got an exception from TokenCountBatchingStrategy ( https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-ai/blob/be0f9fbb676627ec6f64fb99ad9c9cf407ba8941/spring-ai-core/src/main/java/org/springframework/ai/embedding/TokenCountBatchingStrategy.java#L147 ) 

What to do in this situation ?

**Environment**

Spring AI  1.0.0-M4 / jdk21

**Steps to reproduce**

Use vertex embedding with the "auto truncate" option, and test with a large payload.  

**Expected behavior**

Success or at least some way in the documentation to make it works.

**Minimal Complete Reproducible example**

var document = new Document("go ".repeat(50000));
vectorStore.add(List.of(document));

comments.body:
I use pgVector storage. 

If the way is to create a `BatchingStragery`, I guess the documentation on the auto-truncate feature should be explicit. 

Or it is still a work in progress to make it work with embedding models in which case the the case would be corrected later.

I let you close this issue or set a "todo something" if you want.

BTW, do you really want to use auto-truncate?  That means you are likely missing data being embedded. 

 from the docs

```
The API has a maximum input token limit of 20,000. Inputs exceeding this limit result in a 400 error. Each individual input text is further limited to 2048 tokens; any excess is silently truncated. You can also disable silent truncation by setting autoTruncate to false.
```

https://cloud.google.com/vertex-ai/generative-ai/docs/embeddings/get-text-embeddings

I guess if 'auto-truncate' is set to true, then we should not be using any batching strategy.

Thoughts @sobychacko ?

Let says you ingest a complete book in this situation, truncating will take the first chapter to create the embedding  which may be not the relevant part ( it will basically take the "once upon a time .." first sentence ... )  in this situation you may want to split your book in multiple part (I let you imagine how to split this )  and embed those parts one after the other

Now let say I got a corporate knowledge base with multiple page formatted like  "What is the issue,  why it happens,  how to correct"    If the page is too large, the truncate will make the embedding to take  the first 2 parts  and some part of the later ...  and with that I'm totally fine    I got a question from my users, which will likely match a "what is the issue" part, then I will feed the LLM with the full page so with the solution  no big deal to truncate here.

issues.title: ZhipuAI FunctionCalling Error
issues.body: **Bug description** When integrating SpringAI with Zhipu AI to perform function calls, serialization issues occur. Upon inspection, it is found that the serialization configuration in the FunctionTool class of the ZhiPuAiApi is problematic. **Environment** use version is 1.0.0-SNAPSHOT ![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/4e5333ab-1096-48b9-a329-b4b5e6b04613)

comments.body:
when i modify the FunctionTool class, it's run succuess
![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/82e1fa5d-f865-4397-afd8-d069d16f5c39)

I checked the file history and found that it was mistakenly modified in commit [3e9a3fd3b0bb508a07ee7aff5526f35e841b077c](https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-ai/commit/3e9a3fd3b0bb508a07ee7aff5526f35e841b077c).
I am glad that you can find this mistake and fix it

> I am glad that you can find this mistake and fix it

lol, actually, you were the one who found and caught this bug.

issues.title: Refactor VectoStore's usage on Document.getEmbedding
issues.body:
As a result of deprecating `embedding` from the `Document` object, refer: https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-ai/issues/1781, some of the vector store operations, specifically the `doSimilaritySearch` method relies on `Document#getEmbedding`. 

This issue addresses the refactoring of this dependency.
